method_atom: Linear stability analysis of stationary uniform solutions of two nonlinear complex field/wave systems with repulsive self-interaction that are weakly coupled to each other through a barrier (a Josephson-type coupling). The analysis determines the sign of the coupling constant from physical constraints, shows the symmetric ground state is dynamically stable for any coupling strength and interaction, and identifies a long-wavelength transverse (snake-type) instability of an excited configuration carrying a pi relative phase between the two fields.
object_atom: Two barrier-separated, concentric annular (double-ring) Bose-Einstein condensates that are tunnel-coupled through a separating barrier, exhibiting angular-momentum Josephson oscillations.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the coupled-nonlinear-field stability machinery (sign of a weak inter-system coupling, stability of uniform states, snake-type transverse instability of a pi-phase mode); the domain-specific object is the pair of tunnel-coupled annular Bose-Einstein condensates.
